description: Sentinel commands for detecting changes in Claude Code's native permission behavior. Each entry maps to a specific bash-guardrails check that might become redundant as CC evolves.

sentinels.rationale: Here-string with unquoted variable — hook passes through (not auto-approved). Tests CC's native handling of <<< syntax.
Here-string with quoted literal — hook auto-approves. If CC stops prompting for <<<, check 11 becomes unnecessary.
Compound command with &&. CC blocks compound operators to prevent chaining attacks, but cd+git is safe. If CC stops prompting for &&, check 13 compound logic becomes unnecessary.
Semicolon-separated safe commands. If CC stops prompting for ;, check 13 semicolon handling becomes unnecessary.

sentinels.cmd: for f in *.txt; do echo $f; done
sentinels.rationale: Simple for loop with glob iterator. CC flags the ; in loop syntax. If CC learns to parse shell loops natively, the flow-control handling in check 13 becomes unnecessary.

sentinels.cmd: for f in $(find /tmp -name "*.md"); do head -5 "$f"; done
sentinels.rationale: For loop with $(find ...) command substitution — the exact pattern that triggered the bug report. Tests CC's handling of loops + command substitution + semicolons together.

sentinels.cmd: while read -r line; do echo "$line"; done < /dev/null
sentinels.rationale: While-read loop pattern. Very common shell idiom. If CC handles while loops natively, the flow-control handling becomes unnecessary.
If/then/fi conditional. CC flags ; in the syntax. If CC parses conditionals natively, check 13 flow-control handling becomes unnecessary.
Read-only pipeline. CC blocks pipes to prevent command chaining. If CC learns to classify all-readonly pipelines, check 14 becomes unnecessary.
find -exec with readonly command and \;. CC flags the backslash. If CC handles find -exec \; natively, the find-exec path in check 14 becomes unnecessary.
Command matching a typical permissions.allow rule. If CC's built-in allowlist expands to cover common dev tools, check 15 becomes less necessary.
Control: safe single command. Should always be allowed by both hook and CC.
Control: simplest possible command. Verifies the test harness works.
